title: Research Technology Core, College of Health Solutions
authors_full: Chelsea Dorich, Kevin Hollingshead, John Yu, Harsh Anand
authors_short: Chelsea Dorich Kevin Hollingshead John Yu Harsh Anand

summary.hook: This poster describes the Research Technology Core (RTC) at ASU’s College of Health Solutions and how it supports health research through custom mobile apps, participant-management...
summary.summary_30s: This poster describes the Research Technology Core (RTC) at ASU’s College of Health Solutions and how it supports health research through custom mobile apps, participant-management web apps, and integrated wearable/IoT platforms.

talktrack_2m.what_this_is_about: This poster describes the Research Technology Core (RTC) at ASU’s College of Health Solutions and how it supports health research through custom mobile apps...
talktrack_2m.what_they_did: Program/service description with examples and descriptive “key achievements.”. Program totals: 10,000+ participants served; 66 projects deployed; 5 funded projects in pipeline
talktrack_2m.what_they_found: RTC supports at-home, minimally invasive participant engagement via mobile apps and wearable/IoT integrations (Fitbit, CPAP, glucose monitors, Shelly plug).
talktrack_2m.why_it_matters: Researchers can bridge research ideas to real-world health improvements using scalable participant-facing apps plus back-end management tools.
talktrack_2m.one_smart_follow_up: What is the typical engagement workflow with RTC from “ideation to deployment” (intake, scoping, build, testing, maintenance)?

key_numbers: Program totals: 10,000+ participants served; 66 projects deployed; 5 funded projects in pipeline.
takeaways: RTC supports at-home, minimally invasive participant engagement via mobile apps and wearable/IoT integrations (Fitbit, CPAP, glucose monitors, Shelly plug).
The poster includes multiple example product screenshots illustrating: curated education + sleep/diet tracking; participant study oversight dashboards; screen-time usage...
Authors’ stated conclusions: Mobile applications integrated with wearable and IoT technologies provide transformative advantages for health research by enabling real-time...
